name: linear-mcp
title: Linear

setup.blurb: Connect to Linear for issue tracking, project management, sprint cycles, and team coordination.
setup.help_text: Linear's hosted MCP uses OAuth2. On first connection, you'll authorise Sidereal to access your Linear workspace via the browser. No API keys or app creation required.

highlights.text: Issues — create, update, search, and complete across projects
Projects & cycles — track sprints and project milestones
Labels & teams — organise work with team-scoped taxonomy
Inbox & my issues — personal queue and notification triage
OAuth2 — zero-config auth via Linear's hosted flow

readme:
### What it does

Linear MCP is an official vendor-hosted remote server that connects your AI agent to Linear's project management platform. Your agent can search and triage issues, create and update tasks, browse projects and cycles, and manage labels — all through Linear's native API.

Because it runs on Linear's infrastructure, there is no local process to install or maintain. The connection is established via OAuth2 on first use.

### Remote MCP architecture

Unlike most Blade MCPs, Linear runs as a remote server at `mcp.linear.app/mcp`. Sidereal connects directly — no local runtime, no package install, no Python or Node dependency. OAuth2 authentication is handled by Linear's hosted auth flow; you authorise once in the browser and the token is managed automatically.

### Safety model

Linear's MCP supports both read and write operations. Issue creation, updates, and completion are available when the OAuth scopes include `write` and `issues:create`. Read operations (search, list, inbox, team overview) require only the `read` scope.

### Coverage

The service mapping covers the `tasks-v1` contract surface: issues as tasks, projects, labels, inbox, and CRUD operations. Linear's richer concepts — cycles, views, roadmaps, and team structures — are available through the full tool set but extend beyond the contract.

scenarios.label: Triage my Linear inbox
scenarios.body: Pull the personal inbox and current issue assignments, review priority and status, then batch-update issues that are stale or ready to close.

scenarios.label: Create issues from a spec
scenarios.body: Break down a feature specification into discrete Linear issues with labels and project assignment, then verify the team overview reflects the new workload.

scenarios.label: Sprint review summary
scenarios.body: List all issues completed in the current cycle, search for any blocked items, and generate a team overview showing progress against project milestones.

notes: Vendor-hosted remote MCP at mcp.linear.app — no local process. OAuth2 handled by Linear's hosted auth flow. Service mapping is provisional until tools/list is enumerated against the live endpoint. tasks-v1 is a strong fit: issues map to tasks, projects/labels/inbox/create/update/complete all have direct counterparts. Linear's richer concepts (cycles, views, teams, roadmaps) exceed the contract surface.
